steps.title: Use proportional regression in Desmos
steps.content: The choices all have the form $t=kd$, so this is a one-parameter proportional model. Enter distance as $x$ and time as $y$: $x_1=[0.18]$ and $y_1=[9]$. Then type $y_1 \sim kx_1$. Desmos returns $k=50$, so the model is $t=50d$.

steps.title: Write the linear equation
steps.content: With $k = 50$, the equation is $t = 50d$. This means Adriana takes 50 minutes to travel each mile.

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• A: $t = 50d$ — Matches the computed rate of 50 min/mile. That matches choice A — A is correct.
  • B: $t = \frac{1}{2}d$ — At $d = 0.18$: $t = 0.09$ min, not 9 min. Eliminated.
  • C: $t = 2d$ — At $d = 0.18$: $t = 0.36$ min, not 9 min. Eliminated.
  • D: $t = \frac{1}{50}d$ — At $d = 0.18$: $t = 0.0036$ min, not 9 min. Eliminated.
